data.title: REG-Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
ENDEAVOUR ANNOUNCES TRANSACTION IN OWN SHARES

London, 10 April 2025 – Endeavour Mining plc (LSE:EDV, TSX:EDV) (“the Company”) announces it has purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of USD 0.01 each from Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited.

Aggregated information

Dates of purchase:09 April 2025
Aggregate number of ordinary shares of USD 0.01 each purchased:30,000
Lowest price paid per share (GBp):                1,759.01
Highest price paid per share (GBp):        1,780.00
Volume weighted average price paid per share (GBp):        1,767.80

Following the cancellation of the repurchased shares, the Company will have no ordinary shares in treasury and 241,872,483 ordinary shares in issue. Therefore the total voting rights in the Company will be 241,872,483. This figure for the total number of voting rights may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

These share purchases form part of the Company's buy-back programme announced on 20 March 2024.

Transaction details

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), the table below contains detailed information of the individual trades made by Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited as part of the buyback programme.

Schedule of purchases

Shares purchased: Endeavour Mining plc (ISIN: GB00BL6K5J42)

Dates of purchases: 09 April 2025

Investment firm: Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited

ABOUT ENDEAVOUR MINING PLC

Endeavour Mining is one of the world’s senior gold producers and the largest in West Africa, with operating assets across Senegal, Cote d’Ivoire and Burkina Faso and a strong portfolio of advanced development projects and exploration assets in the highly prospective Birimian Greenstone Belt across West Africa.

A member of the World Gold Council, Endeavour is committed to the principles of responsible mining and delivering sustainable value to its employees, stakeholders and the communities where it operates. Endeavour is admitted to listing and to trading on the London Stock Exchange and the Toronto Stock Exchange, under the symbol EDV.

For more information, please visit www.endeavourmining.com.

data.title: He's One of Wall Street's Biggest Bears. Where He's Investing Now.
data.description:
By Paul R. La Monica

David Rosenberg has long been one of the more bearish strategists on Wall Street. At Merrill Lynch in the 2000s, he was one of the few to predict the global financial crisis of 2008-09. Since then, he has largely maintained a negative outlook, intermingled with bullish calls on various assets and markets.

With his reputation as a "permabear," one might think Rosenberg would be taking a victory lap. Financial markets are in turmoil and, despite a surge on Wednesday, the S&P 500 is still down since President Donald Trump announced sweeping additional tariffs on April 3. Volatility has surged and Treasury yields have been spiking as investors worry about a mix of higher inflation and slowing growth — the dreaded specter of "stagflation."

Yet Rosenberg — who runs his own investment firm, Rosenberg Research & Associates, in his hometown of Toronto — does see pockets of strength. He shifted his firm's outlook recently from Bearish to Neutral, citing depressed sentiment and oversold technicals. He isn't upbeat on the S&P 500 seeing the index in a range of 4,600-5,300 over the next 12 months. More attractive, he says, are European defense stocks, Asian equities, and gold.

As for Trump's whipsawing policies, Rosenberg isn't convinced the president's tariff pause will be a game-changer. "Let's not forget that the trade war with China is moving into high gear," he said.

"Households and corporations crave policy stability and aren't receiving it," he added. "If anything, all the president has done is make the overall economic backdrop that much more confusing."

Rosenberg, 64, spoke this week with Barron's about his views on tariffs, the financial markets, and the economy. An edited version of the conversation follows.

Barron's: Your latest stock market call is more optimistic than usual. Why the change?

David Rosenberg: A lot of sentiment metrics in this correction phase have gotten completely washed out. That paves the way for a near-term tradable rally. Whenever the VIX pops above 40 [the volatility measure topped 50 earlier this week], 96% of the time the S&P 500 is up in the ensuing 12 months. If you have that degree of staying power, this [could be] a good buying opportunity.

I'm not convinced that the market lows are in. You may have to hold your nose and withstand a move to a cycle low, which is probably two months away. It depends on your tolerance for short-term pain and how much resolve you have.

Investors don't like Trump's tariffs. Is the White House making a big mistake?

Trump has been opposed to bilateral trade surpluses with the U.S. for almost 40 years. His view on trade, in general, is that other countries' surpluses are an attempt to rip off Americans. For Trump, this is like religion.

I don't view trade surpluses or deficits as good or bad. They're just what they are. I roll my eyes and shake my head when hearing about the perils of running trade deficits. They show the relative strength of the U.S. economy. You should wear that as a badge of courage.

This is a massive assault on a beast otherwise known as global trade. There are going to be repercussions. There are only losers in a trade war, and we have only winners when we have global trade flow. But globalism has become a repulsive term. We're now on the precipice of a global trade war, and that would be catastrophic.

You've written that the market can move to excesses in both directions and make faulty assumptions, but "the market is not stupid." What faulty assumptions are investors making now?

At the time I wrote that [in December 2024], I said I underestimated the power of the artificial-intelligence trade. But the market can go from a mania into a bubble. Stocks, at their peak, were embedding a five-year profile of 20% earnings growth, almost triple the annual average rate of 7%. There is historically a one-in-20 chance of that occurring.

But it wasn't about earnings; it was about the price/earnings multiple. That is the pulse of animal spirits and investor confidence. Did I think we'd get to 23 or 24 times earnings for the market? No.

Heading into the peak, I was wrong in not sharing the market's assumptions. But that was then, and this is now.

What would it take for you to go from being neutral on equities to bullish? When was the last time you were optimistic on the U.S. stock market?

People don't seem to remember that I was very bullish on many segments of the market coming out of the March 2020 lows. I said then that I liked anything remotely connected to tech, such as working from home and deliveries. I also turned bullish from about 2012 to 2017. So, I was in about 60% of that bull market after the global financial crisis.

I may be typically late to a bull market, but I never overstay my welcome. I'm deliberately slow to get in and early to get out. But as a result, in my 40-year career, I've never lost money over a 12-month span.

Were your clients annoyed with you for missing out on some gains?

I've had clients yelling at me that I wasn't being truthful when I liked stocks. They thought it was like taking John Wayne out of a Western and putting him in a romantic comedy. But I was bullish through most of the 1980s and 1990s. By the late '90s, I saw the tech bubble for what it was and became more bearish.

But I am deliberately conservative. I can't stand losing money. Capital preservation is the First Commandment. I was brought up with Depression-era parents who taught me about frugality and savings.

I'll bunt for singles and be happy with the odd double. But I'll never swing for the fences. I don't hit home runs, but I also don't strike out. With today's get-rich-quick philosophy promulgated in so many circles, minimizing drawdowns somehow has labeled me a permabear. That's more a reflection of society than what it says about me.

How much longer do you expect the Federal Reserve to remain on the sidelines before cutting interest rates again?

The Fed is caught in a box between the prospect of much slower growth and a near-term inflation shock. But I've got news for you: If there is no inflation in the jobs market, there is no lasting inflation. The difference between this inflation shock and 2021-22 is that this time we will hit a wall in the labor market.

The Fed is suffering from a case of once burned, twice shy. It will be slow to cut rates again. If not for getting things wrong about inflation being transitory, it would probably be cutting now. But the Fed is consumed with fears about its credibility. [Fed Chair Jerome] Powell's term ends in a year, and given a choice, he'd rather go down presiding over a recession than inflation. Paul Volcker was revered for finally breaking the back of inflation in the 1980s. So, the Fed will be deliberately slow.

When the time comes to cut again, the Fed will be aggressive in the second half of the year. It will be late to the game. That won't be the first time. It will be scrambling to cut rates as odds of a recession have risen materially. Recession risks are high and rising. I know that sounds like 'The Boy Who Cried Wolf." But remember that at the end of that story, the wolf shows up.

Why are recession risks high? The labor market still looks healthy.

Excess savings from Covid are all gone. Besides that, the unprecedented period of fiscal stimulus we had is over, and fiscal policy isn't stimulative due to Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE, cuts and Elon Musk. There is no safety valve or powerful offsets to what is happening now.

Trade policy is being conducted haphazardly. On the one side, some are saying tariffs are permanent; others are saying we'll negotiate. There is this bog of policy uncertainty that is causing us to become more risk-averse. This bog, if it persists, will shave percentage points off gross domestic product for the coming year.

What does all of this mean for other assets, such as international stocks and currencies, bonds, oil, and gold?

Europe and Asia still look superior. European defense stocks are a good place to be. And even though I don't make currency calls too often, we went long the Japanese yen about a month ago. If there is a country in the world that has inflation, it's Japan. Their central bank will be raising rates. You could have equity-like returns being long the yen. That is our highest-conviction call. Asia in general looks attractively priced. It has the valuation support that the U.S. is still lacking.

For bonds, if I am right that the Fed will cut again in the second half of the year, I like the Treasury market, especially longer duration like the 20-year. Oil has gone down due to OPEC+ production and recession concerns. But it is coming closer to an attractive level.

On gold, I've been bullish for years — long before it became front-page news. Our models point to gold being a range-bound trade in 2025, but in the longer term we're still very bullish. Gold could double over the next five years. It is always a ballast in a portfolio, and it is inversely correlated to other assets, so it is an effective hedge.

Also, if you reset global trade and want to talk about the laws of unintended consequences, for the first time in my life, the U.S. could relinquish its reserve currency status. That is on the table.

What would replace the dollar is an open question, but this is the road we are being led down. And if we start to have this reset of global trade, a $6,000 view on gold within the next five years could wind up being a conservative estimate. So, I still like gold. I don't love it, but I probably will end up loving it again.

Thank you, David.

Write to Paul R. La Monica at paul.lamonica@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Skanska replaces Clifton bridge in north England, UK, for GBP 43M, about SEK 580M
data.description:
Skanska

Skanska has signed a contract with Network Rail for its replacement Clifton bridge project in north England, UK. The contract is worth GBP 43M, about SEK 580M, which will be included in the order bookings for Europe for the second quarter 2025.

Skanska will replace the Clifton rail bridge that spans the M6 motorway near Penrith, UK. The existing bridge will be demolished and replaced with a new 130 meter single span steel structure. The new bridge will be built alongside the existing structure, before being installed over a 10-day period early January 2026. Existing bridge support structures will be re-used, reducing programme timescales, cost and resultant carbon emissions.

Construction works have started with the project scheduled for completion in March 2026.

For further information please contact:

David Tuddenham, Senior Communications Business Partner, Skanska UK, tel +44 7880 406018

Andreas Joons, Press Officer, Skanska Group, tel +46 (0)10 449 04 94

Direct line for media, tel +46 (0)10 448 88 99

This and previous releases can also be found at www.skanska.com.

Skanska Group uses its knowledge and foresight to shape the way people live, work, and connect. We are one of the world's largest construction and project development companies, with 2024 revenue totaling SEK 177 billion. We operate across select markets in the Nordics, Europe and USA. Together with our customers and the collective expertise of our approximately 26,300 teammates, we create innovative and sustainable solutions that support healthy living beyond our lifetime.

data.title: Relief Therapeutics Posts Smaller FY24 Net Loss; Revenue Climbs
data.description:
Relief Therapeutics on Thursday reported a smaller loss for full-year 2024, while revenue rose year over year.

Net loss to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 17.1 million francs, compared with a loss of 98.2 million francs earlier. Loss per share moved to 1.365 francs from a loss per share 8.354 francs.

The Swiss biopharmaceutical company's revenue was 8.4 million francs, compared with 6 million francs a year ago.

data.title: SpareBank 1 Helgeland: Purchase of shares in Samarbeidende Sparebanker AS, Samarbeidende Sparebanker Utvikling DA, and SpareBank 1 SamSpar AS
data.description:
The SamSpar alliance consists of the 8 local savings banks (SamSpar banks):SpareBank 1 Gudbrandsdal, SpareBank 1 Hallingdal Valdres, SpareBank 1Helgeland,SpareBank 1 Lom & Skjåk, SpareBank 1 Nordmøre, SpareBank 1 Ringerike Hadeland,SpareBank 1 Østfold Akershus, and SpareBank 1 Sogn og Fjordane. Key figuresfrom2024 show that we have a total business capital of NOK 312 billion and a totalloan volume of NOK 267 billion.

As participants in the SamSpar alliance, the banks engage in a strategiccollaboration on financial and non-financial services and products, includingasparticipants in the SpareBank 1 alliance. The banks jointly own the SamSparcompanies Samarbeidende Sparebanker AS, Samarbeidende Sparebanker UtviklingDA,and SpareBank 1 SamSpar AS, with different ownership ratios in the threejointlyowned companies.

After negotiations, an agreement was reached on April 9, 2025, between theSamSpar banks regarding a reallocation of shares in the jointly ownedcompanies,where SpareBank 1 Helgeland increases its ownership. The transaction involvesSpareBank 1 Helgeland investing a total of NOK 361 million in the threeSamSparcompanies. After the transaction is completed, SpareBank 1 Helgeland will own11.47% of Samarbeidende Sparebanker AS, 11.94% of SpareBank 1 SamSpar AS, and14.81% of Samarbeidende Sparebanker Utvikling DA. This corresponds to anindirect ownership of 2.24% in SpareBank 1 Gruppen AS and 2.67% in SpareBank 1Utvikling DA.

Including the transaction, the pure core capital and total capital adequacyratios as of December 31, 2024, would be 16.74% and 20.81%, respectively, adecrease of 1.06 and 1.08 percentage points.

The transaction will be completed on April 15, 2025.

Mo i Rana, April 10, 2025

Contact persons: Hanne Nordgaard, CEO, tel 481 11 876Anne Ekroll, CFO, tel 913 36 452

data.title: DocMorris Posts Higher Q1 External Revenue
data.description:
DocMorris said Thursday its external revenue increased year over year for the first quarter ended March 31.

The online pharmacy company's revenue was 296.5 million francs, compared with 262.4 million francs previously.

For 2025, the company expects external revenue growth of more than 10%. In the medium term, the compound annual growth rate for external revenue is expected at 20%.

data.title: Relief Therapeutics Reports 2024 Financial Results and Provides Corporate Update
data.description:
GENEVA, SWITZERLAND / ACCESS Newswire / April 10, 2025 / RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA (SIX:RLF)(OTCQB:RLFTF)(OTCQB:RLFTY ) (Relief), a biopharmaceutical company committed to delivering innovative treatment options for select specialty, unmet and rare diseases, today published its 2024 Annual Report and provided a corporate update.

In his annual letter to shareholders, Dr. Raghuram Selvaraju, chairman of the board of directors, emphasized the Company's operational achievements, pipeline momentum, and strengthened financial position. "This past year, we built a stronger foundation and made measurable progress in executing our plans as we advanced our core pipeline and optimized our commercial strategy, always guided by our commitment to delivering meaningful solutions for patients with unmet medical needs," said Dr. Selvaraju. "We are particularly excited by the continued progress of RLF-OD032, our next-generation liquid sapropterin formulation for phenylketonuria, and RLF-TD011, our proprietary hypochlorous acid solution for epidermolysis bullosa. We look forward to important clinical and regulatory milestones in 2025."

Relief's 2024 Annual Report, which includes pipeline and portfolio updates, corporate governance and compensation disclosures, and financial statements, is available on the Company's website .

ABOUT RELIEF

Relief is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to advancing treatment paradigms and improving the lives of patients with rare and debilitating diseases. With core expertise in drug delivery systems and drug repurposing, Relief's clinical pipeline includes innovative treatments designed to address critical unmet medical needs in rare dermatological, metabolic and respiratory conditions. The Company has also successfully brought several approved products to market through licensing and distribution partnerships. Headquartered in Geneva, Relief is listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the symbol RLF and quoted in the U.S. on OTCQB under the symbols RLFTF and RLFTY. For more information, visit www.relieftherapeutics.com

CONTACT

RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA

Jeremy Meinen

Chief Financial Officer

contact@relieftherapeutics.com

DISCLAIMER

This press release contains forward-looking statements, which may be identified by words such as "believe," "assume," "expect," "intend," "may," "could," "will," or similar expressions. These statements are based on current plans and assumptions and are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results, financial condition, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Such factors include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, market developments, regulatory changes, competitive dynamics, and other risks or changes in circumstances. This communication is provided as of the date hereof, and Relief undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements contained herein as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

SOURCE: Relief Therapeutics Holding SA

data.title: UAE's Masdar Fully Acquires Greece's Terna Energy
data.description:
Abu Dhabi Future Energy Co., d/b/a Masdar, closed the purchase of the remaining 30% stake it did not own in Greek renewable energy company Terna Energy (TENERGY.AT).

The Emirati state-owned renewable energy company paid 20 euros per Terna Energy share, valuing the latter at an enterprise value of 3.2 billion euros, according to a Thursday release.

The Abu Dhabi National Energy's (ADX:TAQA), d/b/a Taqa, 43%-owned unit acquired a 70% stake in Terna Energy from Gek Terna and other shareholders in November 2024.

data.title: Cyclopharm Installs Lung Imaging Technology at US Department of Defense Hospital
data.description:
Cyclopharm installed its diagnostic lung imaging technology Technegas at Brooke Army Medical Center (BAMC) in Texas, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Shares of the company rose 2% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: CNOOC Energy Technology & Services 2024 Profit Down 19%, Shares Up 5%
data.description:
CNOOC Energy Technology & Services posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 3.66 billion yuan, down 19% from 3.08 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share fell to 0.3597 yuan from 0.3031 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The petroleum engineering and oilfield technology company's revenue rose 6.5% year over year to 52.5 billion yuan from 49.3 billion yuan.

CNOOC Energy Technology & Services declared a dividend of 0.135 yuan per share.

Shares of the company rose 5% in recent trade.

data.title: American academic charged with insulting Thai monarchy released on bail
data.description:
An American academic who was arrested in Thailand and charged with insulting the royal family has been released on bail, but his immigration status remains contested, his lawyer said on Thursday.

Paul Chambers, a lecturer at Thailand's Naresuan University was detained on Tuesday after a complaint was filed by the royalist army accusing him of defaming the powerful monarchy of Thailand, which has one of the world's strictest lese majeste laws. The Immigration bureau has revoked his visa.

The U.S. State Department had expressed alarm over the arrest, saying it reinforced Washington's longstanding concerns about Thailand's use of the lese majeste law, while urging authorities to "respect freedom of expression and to ensure that laws are not used to stifle permitted expression".

Chambers was released on bail late on Wednesday according to his lawyer, Wannaphat Jenroumjit of Thai Lawyers for Human Rights group. Chamber has also appealed against the revocation of his visa.

"We should hear about the appeal result by Friday," Wannaphat added.

The accusations against Chambers stemmed from a blurb for an online academic seminar at which he was a speaker. The blurb was posted on a website of a research institute based outside of Thailand last year, his lawyer said.

Chambers will not be deported from Thailand until the court process is completed, an immigration officer said on the condition of anonymity because he was not authorised to speak to media.

Thailand's monarchy is protected by Section 112 of the country's penal code, which says anyone found guilty of defaming, insulting or threatening the king, queen, heir apparent or regent shall be punished with imprisonment of three to 15 years.

data.title: BOJ Likely to Keep Rates Steady in May to Assess Tariff ImpactMarket Talk
data.description: With a high degree of uncertainty surrounding U.S. trade policy likely to persist through early July, the Bank of Japan is expected to hold interest rates steady at its next meeting on April 30 and May 1, Morgan Stanley MUFG Securities economists say. "The BOJ will need to assess the negative impact of the 25% autos tariff on SME [small and medium enterprise} wage negotiations taking place over April-June," they say in a research note. The economists see the next rate increase as most likely in September, with some probability of a rate rise in July, depending on forex moves and the upper house election results. They expect the BOJ to pause monetary tightening after raising the policy rate to 0.75% from the current 0.5%. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Russia downs 48 Ukrainian drones, defence ministry says
data.description:
Russian air defence systems destroyed 48 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions overnight and in the morning on Thursday, Russia's Defence Ministry said.

Thirteen drones were downed each in Bryansk region and Kursk region, twelve drones - over Kaluga region, the other were shot down in Belgorod, Moscow and Oryol regions and over Russia-annexed Crimea.

data.title: Barry Callebaut trims volume outlook on high cocoa price volatility
data.description:
Swiss chocolate maker Barry Callebaut lowered its annual volume guidance on Thursday due to "unprecedented volatility" in cocoa bean prices.

The world's biggest chocolatier, which supplies key food producers such as KitKat maker Nestle , forecast a mid-single-digit percentage decrease in its cocoa sales volume for the financial year ending on August 31.

It had previously said it expected the cocoa sales volume to fall by a low single-digit percentage, amid the exceptionally high prices of the raw material that trades in London at around 5,722 pounds ($7,343) per metric ton .

data.title: New Zealand to Work with Like-Minded Countries to Promote Free Trade, Prime Minister Says
data.description:
New Zealand will continue to work with like-minded countries to promote free trade and it will explore the role of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) agreement in this regard, the country's Prime Minister Christopher Luxon said in a speech to the Wellington Chamber of Commerce on Thursday.

One possibility is that members of the CPTPP and the European Union collaborate to support rules-based trade, making specific commitments on how that support plays out in practice, he said. This could include action to prevent restrictions on exports and efforts to ensure any retaliation is consistent with existing rules.

Export growth will be critical to improving New Zealand's economic prospects in the coming years, while the removal of New Zealand's trade barriers import embrace of goods and services from offshore has led to a major improvement in quality of life in the country in recent years, Luxon added.

The direct impact on New Zealand's economy from the tariffs declared by US President Donald Trump last week is likely to be around NZ$900 million, or roughly 0.2% of its GDP.

It is also negotiating a free-trade agreement with India. The government intends to deepen its partnerships with countries in Southeast Asia, South Korea, Japan, and the US.

data.title: Swiss stocks - Factors to watch on April 10
data.description:
Here are some of the main factors that may affect Swiss stocks on Thursday:

TARIFFS

Swiss President Karin Keller-Sutter said on Wednesday she had spoken to U.S. President Donald Trump by telephone about trade and was looking forward to reaching agreements after Switzerland was hit with bigger import tariffs than its EU neighbours.

BARRY CALLEBAUT

The Swiss chocolate maker lowered its annual volume guidance on Thursday due to "unprecedented volatility" in cocoa bean prices.

GIVAUDAN

The Swiss fragrance and flavour maker on Thursday reported quarterly sales slightly above market forecasts and said it was highly likely to exceed its five-year growth target until end-2025.

data.title: Givaudan Sales Grow; Warns of Rising Prices
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Givaudan said that first-quarter sales grew but warned of higher prices caused by growing costs and tariffs which will be passed on to customers.

The Swiss flavor-and-fragrance company said Thursday that first-quarter sales rose 8.5% to 1.98 billion Swiss francs ($2.31 billion) compared with the same period a year prior.

Givaudan said it is raising prices due to higher costs and tariffs, and intends for the price increases to fully compensate for the rise in input costs.

Givaudan remains focused on delivering its strategy despite uncertainty caused by global trade tariffs, the company's Chief Executive Gilles Andrier said.

The company said it has started the year with strong momentum, delivering growth across markets and segments. Sales in high-growth markets continued to grow but at a slower pace, increasing 12.8% on a like-for-like basis compared with 21.8% a year earlier.

Givaudan backed its sales targets and said it expects to exceed the upper end of its average five-year sales growth target of 4%-5% on a like-for-like basis for the period 2021-25. It had averaged like-for-like sales growth of 7.2% between 2021 and 2024, it said.

Sales within its fragrance-and-beauty division increased 12.2% to 1.01 billion Swiss francs, and rose 5% to 968 million Swiss francs in its taste-and-wellbeing division.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Lytix Biopharma AS: Notice of Annual General Meeting on 29 April 2025
data.description:
Lytix Biopharma AS

Oslo, 10 April 2024, the Board of Directors hereby calls for the Annual General Meeting of Lytix Biopharma AS (the "Company") in Sandakerveien 130, 0484 Oslo, Norway at 13:00 Hours (CEST) on 29 April 2025.

All documents regarding the Annual General Meeting are available at the Company's website: www.lytixbiopharma.com.

For further information, please contact:

Gjest Breistein, CFO

E-mail: gjest.breistein@lytixbiopharma.com

Phone: +47 952 60 512

About Lytix

Based in Oslo, Norway, Lytix Biopharma is a clinical-stage biotech company with a highly novel technology based on world leading research in host-defense peptide-derived molecules. Lytix Biopharma's lead product, LTX-315, is a first-in-class oncolytic molecule representing a new principle to boost anti-cancer immunity. Lytix Biopharma has a pipeline of molecules that can work in many different cancer indications and treatment settings, both as mono- and combination therapy.

data.title: Investors react to intensifying Sino-US trade tensions
data.description:
U.S. Donald Trump ratcheted up a trade war with China even as he did an about turn on steep new reciprocal tariffs on most trading partners on Wednesday.

Trump hiked the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% from the 104% level that kicked in on Wednesday.

Beijing may again respond in kind after slapping 84% tariffs on U.S. imports on Wednesday to match Trump's earlier tariff salvo. It has repeatedly vowed to "fight to the end" in the escalating trade war between the world's top two economies.

MARKET REACTION: The blue-chip CSI 300 Index and Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index both traded higher, in anticipation of domestic policy stimulus to offset tariffs and state-driven buying of stocks.

COMMENTS:

HONG HAO, CEO AT HUAFU INTERNATIONAL, HONG KONG

"Right now, it's mainly a technical rebound. Obviously, Trump backed down yesterday. At the same time, he also expressed openness to negotiate with China. On China's side, it's very clear that we have a lot of confidence this time. Whether it's the announcement of retaliation or the deployment of policies, it's actually all been well-prepared in advance.

"After seven years of experience since 2018, the proportion of U.S. in China's total exports has significantly decreased. So, we're no longer as passive as we were back then. I think this rebound will continue for a while."

SAT DUHRA, PORTFOLIO MANAGER AT JANUS HENDERSON INVESTORS, SINGAPORE

"The volatility is not over, the environment remains uncertain and some of the impacts are irreversible.

"The discussions with China now appear to be more protracted than initial expectations - we have not reduced China given that we are seeing a more fundamental improvement and there are many levers that China can use in mitigation.

"We are generally neutral weight. We only like two areas in China - cheap high growth China internet names and high yield state-owned enterprise (SOE) attractively valued names."

LIAM ZHOU, FOUNDER OF MINORITY ASSET MANAGEMENT, SHANGHAI

"Trump's wanton policies will inevitably face internal and external pressure, so they're not sustainable. Higher tariffs won't address issues such as the trade deficit, the manufacturing sector's hollowing out, and government debt problems. Instead, it will trigger U.S. economic recession, and dent U.S. sovereign creditability.

"China stocks have limited room to fall, as valuations are relatively low, and China has in recent years reduced economic reliance on the U.S., contained property risks, upgraded manufacturing and made technological breakthroughs."

JASON CHAN, SENIOR INVESTMENT STRATEGIST AT BANK OF EAST ASIA, HONG KONG

"Even though it's obvious that the tariffs are targeting China, there is still some room for manoeuvring and negotiations if they can pause tariffs on other countries. Markets still have some hope that at least some discussions could take place. China is also likely to roll out stimulus measures to support the economy and counter the impact of the tariffs in the near future.

"Will the tariffs be rolled back to the level before? The chances of that happening are quite slim. Therefore, even if the market sees some short-term gains, I don't think it will quickly return to previous levels or experience a significant rebound."

CHARU CHANANA, CHIEF INVESTMENT STRATEGIST, SAXO, SINGAPORE

"While this pause does not extend to China, the overall positive sentiment has had a spillover effect, boosting investor confidence in Asian markets, including Hong Kong.

"Investor optimism around a potential US-China deal has also resurfaced after President Trump suggested tariffs on China may have peaked ... Meanwhile, expectations remain high for continued domestic stimulus from Chinese authorities to support growth.

"However, several uncertainties remain and the trade-weighted effective tariff rate is still jumping higher. There is also likely to be plenty of volatility in the coming weeks as negotiation headlines take centre stage."

data.title: Al Ansari Financial Services Finalizes Purchase of BFC Group
data.description:
Al Ansari Financial Services (DFM:ALANSARI) finalized its $200 million purchase of Bahrain-based foreign exchange and remittance company BFC Group.

The transaction was completed after obtaining all required approvals from regulatory authorities, according to a Thursday release. It disclosed the deal in July 2024.

With the acquisition, the United Arab Emirates-based financial services company is set to have the largest branch network in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, boosting its market position, portfolio, and geographic reach.

data.title: Barry Callebaut Posts Lower Fiscal H1 Attributable Net Profit; Revenue Rises
data.description:
Barry Callebaut said Thursday net profit for the fiscal first half decreased while revenue increased year over year.

Net profit attributable to shareholders for the six months ended Feb. 28 was 32.1 million francs, compared with 77.9 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 5.82 francs from 14.20 francs.

The chocolate and cocoa products company's revenue was 7.29 billion francs, compared with 4.64 billion francs a year ago.

For the full fiscal 2025, the company expects a mid-single-digit growth in sales volume.
